question: Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
answer: The current Secretary-General of the United Nations is António Guterres of Portugal [1].
retrieved_contexts:
A United Nations Secretary-General selection is currently being held to choose the next Secretary-General of the United Nations. The incumbent Secretary-General is António Guterres of Portugal, whose term ends on 31 December 2026.


Campaign

On 16 January 2026, Annalena Baerbock, President of the United Nations General Assembly, publicly called on more candidates to enter the race.


Candidates


Registered candidates
The selection process formally began on 25 November 2025. To date, five candidates have been nominated, of whom one subsequently withdrew.


Withdrawn


Expressed interest but no formal candidacy filed


Speculated


Declined
 Juan Manuel Santos, President of Colombia (2010–2018) (endorsed a female candidate)


Endorsements


Notes


References


External links
"Selection and appointment of the next Secretary-General". United Nations.

The secretary-general of the United Nations (UNSG or UNSECGEN) is the chief administrator of the United Nations who oversees the United Nations Secretariat, one of the six principal organs of the United Nations, it also represents all 193 nations on the global stage.
The role of the secretary-general and of the secretariat is laid out by Chapter XV (Articles 97 to 101) of the United Nations Charter. However, the office's qualifications, selection process and tenure are open to interpretation; they have been established by custom.


Selection and term of office

Powers and duties
The UN Charter designates the secretary-general as the "chief administrative officer" of the UN, and gives the secretary-general the power to make employment decisions about Secretariat staff. The Charter also empowers the secretary-general to inform the Security Council of "any matter which in his opinion may threaten the maintenance of international peace and security", and allows the secretary-general to perform "such other functions as are entrusted" by other United Nations organs. These provisions have been interpreted as providing broad leeway for officeholders to serve a variety of roles as suited to their preferences, skill set, or circumstances. The UN describes the role of the secretary-general as combining the functions and responsibilities of an advocate, diplomat, civil servant, and chief executive officer.
The secretary-general's routine duties include overseeing the activities and duties of the secretariat; attending sessions with United Nations bodies; consulting with world leaders, government officials, and other stakeholders; and travelling the world to engage with global constituents and bring attention to certain international issues. The secretary-general publishes an annual report on the work of the UN, which includes an assessment of its activities and an outline future priorities. The secretary-general is also the chairman of the United Nations System Chief Executives Board for Coordination (CEB), a body composed of the heads of all UN funds, programmes, and specialised agencies, which meets twice a year to discuss substantive and management issues facing the United Nations System.
Many of the secretary-general's powers are informal and left open to individual interpretation; some appointees have opted for more activist roles, while others have been more technocratic or administrative. The secretary-general is often reliant upon the use of their "good offices", described as "steps taken publicly and in private, drawing upon his independence, impartiality and integrity, to prevent international disputes from arising, escalating or spreading". Consequently, observers have variably described the office as the "world's most visible bully pulpit" or as the "world's moderator". Examples include Dag Hammarskjöld's promotion of an armistice between the warring parties of Arab-Israel conflict, Javier Perez de Cuellar's negotiation of a ceasefire in the Iran-Iraq War, and U Thant's role in deescalating the Cuban Missile Crisis.
reference_answer: António Guterres is the Secretary-General of the United Nations.
reference_contexts: The Secretary-General of the United Nations is António Guterres, who assumed office on 1 January 2017.
António Guterres serves as the ninth Secretary-General of the United Nations.
António Guterres, Portuguese politician and diplomat, has been UN Secretary-General since 2017.

question: Which company currently has the largest market capitalization in the world?
answer: In 2026, NVIDIA holds the top position as the world's most valuable company by market capitalization, largely due to its leadership in artificial intelligence and semiconductors [1].
retrieved_contexts:
Published on: 2026-03-20
The largest companies by market capitalisation are among the most influential and profitable firms in the global economy. Market capitalisation, or market cap, is calculated by multiplying a company’s share price by the total number of its outstanding shares. It reflects what investors believe the company is worth and gives a snapshot of its size relative to global peers. In 2026, advanced technology giants dominate the top rankings, illustrating how digital innovation drives corporate value in global markets.
NVIDIA leads the world in market cap in 2026, driven by its leadership in artificial intelligence and semiconductors.
Technology firms dominate the rankings, with multiple companies exceeding $3 trillion in market capitalization, reflecting investor confidence in the tech sector.
Market cap is dynamic, driven by share price movements, earnings results, industry shifts, and investor sentiment.
Beyond tech, energy and diversified industrial companies also appear in broader top rankings.
These companies influence markets, employment, technological progress, and economic trends worldwide.
Here is a snapshot of the top 10 most valuable companies globally based on market cap in Q1 2026, showing what industries and geographies lead the global economic landscape:
Notes: Market cap figures fluctuate daily based on share prices and corporate actions. This ranking reflects Q1 2026 data.
NVIDIA has risen rapidly to become the world’s most valuable company by market cap in 2026. It owes this position to its critical role in artificial intelligence, graphics processing units (GPUs), and data centre technology. The company’s chips power high‑performance computing, AI model training, autonomous vehicle systems, and cloud infrastructure, making it a backbone of global technological innovation.
Apple remains one of the most influential consumer technology companies in the world. Its ecosystem of products, including the iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and expanding services like Apple TV+ and Apple Music, generates recurring revenue and strong customer loyalty. Apple’s scale and brand prestige contribute to its multi‑trillion‑dollar market cap.
Alphabet, the parent company of Google, has benefited from explosive growth in digital advertising, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence initiatives such as its Gemini AI platform. Strong performance in search and cloud services has helped it consistently rank near the top of global market cap charts.

Market capitalization, sometimes referred to as market cap, is the total value of a publicly traded company's outstanding common shares owned by stockholders. 
Market capitalization is equal to the market price per common share multiplied by the number of common shares outstanding.


Description
Market capitalization is sometimes used to rank the size of companies. It measures only the equity component of a company's capital structure, and does not reflect management's decision as to how much debt (or leverage) is used to finance the firm. A more comprehensive measure of a firm's size is enterprise value (EV), which gives effect to outstanding debt, preferred stock, and other factors. For insurance firms, a value called the embedded value (EV) has been used.
It is also used in ranking the relative size of stock exchanges, being a measure of the sum of the market capitalizations of all companies listed on each stock exchange. The total capitalization of stock markets or economic regions may be compared with other economic indicators (e.g. the Buffett indicator). The approximate total market capitalization of all publicly traded companies in the USA was:

2023: US$48.98 trillion
2024: US$62.19 trillion
October 3rd 2025: US$69.244 trillion


Historical estimates of world market cap
Total market capitalization of all publicly traded companies in the world from 1975:


Calculation
Market cap is given by the formula 
  
    
      
        
          MC
        
        =
        N
        ×
        P
      
    
    {\textstyle {\text{MC}}=N\times P}
  
, where MC is the market capitalization, N is the number of common shares outstanding, and P is the market price per common share.
For example, if a company has 4 million common shares outstanding, and the closing price per share is $20, its market capitalization is then $80 million. If the closing price per share rises to $21, the market cap becomes $84 million. If it drops to $19 per share, the market cap falls to $76 million. This is in contrast to mercantile pricing where purchase price, average price and sale price may differ due to transaction costs.
Not all of the outstanding shares trade on the open market.  The number of shares trading on the open market is called the float.  It is equal to or less than N because N includes shares that are restricted from trading.  The free-float market cap uses just the floating number of shares in the calculation, generally resulting in a smaller number.

Microsoft’s strength lies in its diversified software ecosystem, cloud services (Azure), enterprise solutions, and productivity tools. Its recurring revenue model, enterprise market penetration, and investments in AI continue to attract investor confidence.
Amazon’s dominant position in e‑commerce and cloud computing through Amazon Web Services (AWS) ensures its place among the most valuable companies. Cloud services contribute a large portion of profits, while logistics and digital retail keep their business model resilient.
TSMC is the world’s largest dedicated semiconductor foundry. It produces chips for major technology firms and plays a critical role in the global supply chain for advanced processors. Its high valuation reflects the strategic importance of semiconductor manufacturing to modern economies.
Saudi Aramco remains the most valuable energy company globally, producing vast quantities of oil and gas. Its strong cash flows and strategic position in the Middle East underpin its large market cap.
Meta, formerly Facebook, remains a social media powerhouse with billions of users worldwide across platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. Growth in virtual reality and metaverse‑related projects adds to its long‑term potential.
Broadcom is a major semiconductor and infrastructure software company. Its chips are used in networking, broadband, and enterprise systems, and its strategic acquisitions have contributed to its rising valuation.
Tesla is a leading electric vehicle maker and also a key player in renewable energy solutions. Its innovation in EV technology, battery storage, and autonomous systems drives investor interest and supports its multibillion‑dollar market cap.
The largest companies by market cap in 2026 are overwhelmingly in the technology and semiconductor sectors. These industries benefit from outsized growth prospects, scalable digital platforms, and recurring revenue models, which attract strong investor demand.
Artificial intelligence has become a key growth driver across the largest companies. Firms that lead in AI innovation, data processing, and cloud computing typically command premium valuations.
Many of the top companies now represent not just economic value but also global influence. Their technologies, products, and services shape industries, consumer behaviour, and digital infrastructure worldwide.
Market capitalisation represents the total dollar value of a company’s outstanding shares and reflects investor valuation of its growth potential and future earnings.
Technology companies often have scalable business models, high profit margins, and strong recurring revenues, which support higher valuations relative to traditional industries.
Yes. Market cap rankings can shift daily due to stock price movement, earnings results, and investor sentiment.
reference_answer: NVIDIA Corporation has recently ranked as one of the companies with the highest global market capitalization, frequently alternating with Apple depending on market conditions.
reference_contexts: NVIDIA and Apple have both occupied the top position in global market capitalization rankings in recent periods.
NVIDIA briefly surpassed Apple to become the most valuable public company amid AI-driven stock growth.
Market leadership by capitalization has shifted between major tech firms including Apple, Microsoft, and NVIDIA.

question: How does studying computer science in Australia compare to studying in Canada for international students?
answer: Both Australia and Canada have robust technology sectors, making them attractive destinations for international students pursuing computer science. Australia is particularly strong in cybersecurity and fintech, with hubs in Sydney and Melbourne. Canada offers proximity to Silicon Valley and has significant AI research clusters in Toronto and Montreal. Both countries list IT occupations on their skilled migration lists, which can aid in obtaining permanent residency [2].
retrieved_contexts:
Study in Australia vs Canada — Independent Comparison Guide
Australia and Canada are two of the most popular destinations for international students, and for good reason. Both offer high-quality education, multicultural cities, post-study work rights, and pathways to permanent residency. But the two countries differ in important ways — from climate and cost to visa rules and university rankings. This guide compares them side by side so you can make an informed choice.
At a Glance
| Criteria | Australia | Canada |
|---|---|---|
| Universities in QS Top 100 | 7 | 3 |
| Average annual tuition | AUD $25,000 - $45,000 | CAD $20,000 - $35,000 |
| Post-study work visa | Subclass 485 — 2 to 4 years | PGWP — up to 3 years |
| PR pathway | General Skilled Migration | Express Entry |
| Official language | English | English & French |
| Climate | Warm to tropical | Cold winters, warm summers |
| Health cover | OSHC required | Provincial health — varies by province |
University Rankings Comparison
Australia punches well above its weight in global rankings. Seven Australian universities sit inside the QS World Top 100 — the University of Melbourne, the University of Sydney, UNSW Sydney, the Australian National University, Monash University, the University of Queensland, and the University of Western Australia. Canada has three in the same bracket: the University of Toronto, McGill University, and the University of British Columbia. Both countries maintain rigorous quality assurance frameworks. Australia's Tertiary Education Quality and Standards Agency (TEQSA) and Canada's provincial accreditation bodies ensure degrees meet high standards. For students focused on research-intensive programmes or specific fields like mining engineering, marine science, or AI, both countries have globally recognised centres of excellence.
Tuition Fee Comparison
Average international tuition in Australia ranges from AUD $25,000 to $45,000 per year, depending on the course and institution. In Canada, fees typically fall between CAD $20,000 and $35,000. Medicine, dentistry, and MBA programmes sit at the higher end in both countries. Keep in mind that exchange rates fluctuate — at times the real cost difference narrows considerably. Some Australian universities offer merit-based tuition fee waivers of 10-25%, and Canada similarly offers international student scholarships. Always compare the net cost after any scholarships or fee reductions you may be eligible for.

This is general information for comparison purposes only. Immigration rules change frequently in both countries. For advice on your specific situation, consult a registered migration agent (for Australia: mara.gov.au) or equivalent regulated adviser for Canada.
Both Australia and Canada offer points-based skilled migration systems that favour young, qualified graduates with local work experience. Australia's General Skilled Migration programme uses occupation lists that are updated regularly — certain occupations in healthcare, IT, and engineering tend to remain in demand. State and territory nomination can boost your points. Canada's Express Entry system draws from three economic immigration programmes and has recently introduced category-based draws targeting specific occupations. Having Canadian education and work experience provides significant additional points. In both countries, the pathway from student visa to PR typically takes several years and is not guaranteed — it depends on your occupation, experience, English proficiency, and the policy settings at the time you apply.
Which Is Better for Your Field?
computerIT & Computer Science
Both countries have thriving tech sectors. Australia's strength lies in cybersecurity and fintech hubs in Sydney and Melbourne. Canada offers proximity to Silicon Valley and strong AI research clusters in Toronto and Montreal. Both list IT occupations on their skilled migration lists.
health_and_safetyNursing & Healthcare
Australia has acute nursing shortages and offers streamlined registration pathways through AHPRA. Nursing is consistently on Australia's priority skilled occupation list. Canada also faces healthcare workforce gaps, particularly in rural provinces. Both countries offer strong PR prospects for healthcare graduates.
businessBusiness & Management
Australia's business schools are highly ranked in the Asia-Pacific region, with strong industry ties in finance, consulting, and trade. Canada's MBA programmes benefit from proximity to the US market. Both countries have post-study work options for business graduates, though PR pathways may be more competitive outside shortage occupations.
engineeringEngineering
Australia's mining, civil, and environmental engineering programmes rank among the world's best, driven by the country's resources sector. Canada is strong in petroleum, aerospace, and clean energy engineering. Engineering occupations appear on skilled migration lists in both countries, making PR pathways accessible for graduates.
Related Guides
Need help deciding between Australia and Canada?
Our free advisory service connects you with qualified education agents who specialise in Australian universities. Get personalised course and visa guidance.

Cost of Living Comparison
Living costs depend heavily on which city you choose. Sydney and Melbourne are among the more expensive cities in Australia, comparable to Toronto and Vancouver in Canada. Regional cities in both countries — such as Adelaide, Hobart, Winnipeg, or Halifax — are significantly cheaper. In Australia, the Department of Home Affairs uses AUD $29,710 per year as its financial capacity benchmark for student visa applicants. Canada's current minimum is approximately CAD $20,635 per year (outside Quebec). In practice, most students spend more than these minimums, especially in major cities. Shared accommodation, cooking at home, and using public transport are key strategies for managing costs in both destinations.
Post-Study Work Rights Comparison
This is general information for comparison purposes only. Immigration rules change frequently in both countries. For advice on your specific situation, consult a registered migration agent (for Australia: mara.gov.au) or equivalent regulated adviser for Canada.
Australia's Temporary Graduate visa (Subclass 485) gives graduates 2 to 4 years of post-study work rights, depending on their qualification level and whether they studied in a regional area. Graduates with qualifications in areas of verified skill shortage may receive additional time. Canada's Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) offers up to 3 years of open work rights, with the duration linked to program length. Both permits allow full-time work in any occupation, giving graduates time to gain local experience and potentially transition to a permanent visa. The generosity of these programmes fluctuates with policy changes, so always check current settings before making your decision.
Permanent Residency Pathways — General Comparison
This is general information for comparison purposes only. Immigration rules change frequently in both countries. For advice on your specific situation, consult a registered migration agent (for Australia: mara.gov.au) or equivalent regulated adviser for Canada.
reference_answer: Australia and Canada both offer strong computer science programs, but Canada often has a slight advantage in post-study work opportunities, while Australia is known for structured coursework and strong industry alignment.
reference_contexts: Australia offers post-study work visas and industry-focused degrees with structured learning pathways.
Canada provides Post-Graduation Work Permits (PGWP) that allow graduates to gain Canadian work experience.
Both countries host globally ranked universities in computer science and engineering fields.
Canada is often preferred for immigration pathways; Australia is preferred for structured academic support.
